## Quotation candidates (auto excerpt)

**gb-08 (excerpt):** - Virgil (anti-Homer) frames piety/obedience as the central force, explicitly opposing Homer’s love-as-unifier. - The Aeneid as Roman foundation myth: love is abandoned to fulfill the gods’ plan and found Rome (later aligned with the Catholic Church and Dante as the antidote). - Juno/Jupiter conflict: Aeneas is pulled into love with Dido, then commanded to abandon love via Mercury’s intervention. …
